title: Reporters Talk Through FDA Sunscreen Move and Closure of Rural Dialysis Clinics
summary: KFF Health News journalists made the rounds on national media last week to discuss topical stories. Here’s a collection of their appearances.

title: Trouble Getting Weight Loss Drugs Covered by Insurance? Here’s What To Know
summary: If your doctor prescribes a GLP-1 medication for weight loss but your insurance won’t cover it, you have options.

title: Efforts To End School Vaccine Mandates Hit a Wall in Florida
summary: The state’s campaign to end school vaccine requirements is dead for now. The reasons could offer insights into similar efforts’ chances in other states.

title: Medicare Advantage Company Pays $342M to Government in Midst of Billing Probe
summary: The payment by Elevance Health to the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services comes as the agency threatened to bar new enrollments in the company’s plans.

title: Opioid Settlement Money Pays for Services To Battle Addiction in Rural Kentucky
summary: A program in rural eastern Kentucky is receiving opioid settlement funding to address substance use disorders, housing, hunger, and other challenges.

title: Democrats To Propose Bill Capping Out-of-Pocket Medicare Costs for Enrollees
summary: Some Senate Democrats want to cap the amount beneficiaries in traditional Medicare have to pay toward care, but the move is expected to draw GOP opposition for potentially adding billions to Medicare costs.

title: Even in Blue States, Hospitals Have Continued To Drop Gender-Affirming Care for Youths
summary: Massachusetts passed laws and joined lawsuits to protect access to gender-affirming care for minors. But faced with the Trump administration’s threats, some hospitals voluntarily stopped care. Families are outraged.

title: Worried About Your Aging Parents? Welcome to the Caregiving Club
summary: Being a caregiver can start long before you go to a doctor appointment with a loved one or move your parents into your house. The HealthQ team explores how embracing the role matters — and how the recognition and support that come next can ease a difficult season of life.

title: Medicare’s AI Push Snarls Patients and Doctors in Errors and Delays
summary: Medicare is testing the use of artificial intelligence to preapprove several healthcare services. Federal health officials say prior authorization can help reduce fraud and contain costs. But doctors and patients describe the trial as “horrendous” and full of red tape so far.

title: Indiana Takes On Powerful Hospitals by Capping Prices They Charge Employers
summary: “Government has to intervene, because healthcare is run like an unregulated utility,” Indiana’s GOP governor says of the state’s effort to regulate hospital prices.
